PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM CALIBRATION (a) Perform calibration    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NOTICE: If a DTC is output after replacing the master cylinder solenoid, it is possible that calibration has not been performed or the zero point calibration data was not cleared when calibration was performed. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TEST MODE DTC (a) Switch the vehicle to test mode, perform the downhill assist control switch or crawl switch signal check, and then check that DTC C1379 is cleared    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. Result B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  GO TO DTC C1379  See: C1379

dtcs.description: BRAKE CONTROL / DYNAMIC CONTROL SYSTEMS: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM: C0226, C0236, C0246, C0256, C1225-C1228: SFR Solenoid Circuit

STR Solenoid Circuit The solenoid turns on when signals are received from the skid control ECU and controls the pressure acting on the wheel cylinders, thus controlling braking force. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: RECONFIRM DTC HINT These codes are stored when a problem is detected in the master cylinder solenoid. The solenoid circuit is in the master cylinder solenoid. Therefore, solenoid circuit inspections and solenoid unit inspections cannot be performed. Be sure to check if any DTC is output before replacing the master cylinder solenoid. (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Drive the vehicle at a speed of approximately 32 km/h (20 mph) or more for 60 seconds or more. (c) Check if the same DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE MASTER CYLINDER SOLENOID  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

dtcs.description: Engine Control System Malfunction If a malfunction is detected in the engine control system, the ECM sends a malfunction signal to the skid control ECU. The skid control ECU stores this DTC and prohibits the following controls: * VSC * TRAC * Downhill assist control * Crawl control * Multi-terrain select control PROCEDURE s/SFI System A -- REPLACE ECM See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: ECM Communication Circuit Malfunction The circuit is used to send control information from the skid control ECU to the ECM, and engine control information from the ECM to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (ENGINE CONTROL SYSTEM)   See: C1201 B --  GO TO ENGINE CONTROL SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Proceed with A --  REPLACE ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Zero Point Calibration of Yaw Rate Sensor Undone

Zero Point Calibration of Acceleration Sensor Undone The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (yaw rate sensor communication trouble) and U0124 (acceleration sensor communication trouble) are stored. The DTCs are also stored when the calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM TEST MODE (SIGNAL CHECK) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Perform the signal check in the Test Mode Procedure    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. OK: All test mode DTCs are cleared. NG --  CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION OK --  END

CHECK YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has been installed properly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Installation. OK: The sensor is tightened to the specified torque. The sensor is not installed at an angle. NG --  INSTALL YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR CORRECTLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Installation OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Data List. ABS/VSC/TRAC (f) Check that the yaw rate and acceleration sensor output value is displayed on the Techstream. OK: The yaw rate and acceleration sensor output value is normal. NOTICE: Check the yaw rate and acceleration sensor signal after replacement    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. NG --  REPLACE YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Acceleration/Deceleration Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  REPLACE MASTER CYLINDER SOLENOID  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

Acceleration Sensor Output Voltage Malfunction (Test Mode DTC) The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. BRAKE CONTROL / DYNAMIC CONTROL SYSTEMS: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM: C1232, C1243, C1245, C1279: Acceleration Sensor Stuck Malfunction

Open in Pump Motor Circuit The motor relay (semiconductor relay) is built into the master cylinder solenoid and drives the pump motor based on a signal from the skid control ECU. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT Remove the hydraulic brake booster assembly before the inspection See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Testing and Inspection/On-Vehicle Inspection. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTION OF PUMP MOTOR WIRE HARNESS (a) Remove the hydraulic brake booster assembly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Check the tightening torque of the 2 screws which secure the wire harness connecting the master cylinder solenoid and brake booster with accumulator pump assembly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Reassembly. OK: The harness is tightened to the specified torque. Torque : 2.9 Nm (30 kgf-cm, 26 in-lbf) NG --  RETIGHTEN SCREWS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK RESISTANCE OF PUMP MOTOR WIRE HARNESS (a) Using a screwdriver, remove the 2 screws and pull out the wire harness from the master cylinder solenoid. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE BRAKE BOOSTER WITH ACCUMULATOR PUMP ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Reassemble the hydraulic brake booster assembly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Reassembly. (b) Install the hydraulic brake booster assembly    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Installation. (c) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Turn the ignition switch off. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. dtcs.description: Brake Booster Pump Motor on Time Abnormally Long The motor relay (semiconductor relay) is built into the master cylinder solenoid and drives the pump motor based on a signal from the skid control ECU. HINT The pump motor continues operation for the first 3 minutes, and then starts and stops repeatedly. NOTICE: When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT When DTC C1253, C1254, C1256 or C1452 is output together with DTC C1252, inspect and repair the trouble area indicated by DTC C1253, C1254, C1256 or C1452 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK PUMP MOTOR OPERATION (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Depress the brake pedal more than 40 times. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Check how the pump motor operates. Result B --  REPLACE HYDRAULIC BRAKE BOOSTER ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal C --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ACCUMULATOR SENSOR) D --  RECONFIRM DTC A -- Continue to next step.
CHECK CONNECTION OF PUMP MOTOR WIRE HARNESS   See: C1251 NG --  RETIGHTEN SCREWS OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK RESISTANCE OF PUMP MOTOR WIRE HARNESS   See: C1251 NG --  REPLACE BRAKE BOOSTER WITH ACCUMULATOR PUMP ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ACCUMULATOR SENSOR) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Data List. ABS/VSC/TRAC (f) Check the accumulator output value. Result B --  REPLACE MASTER CYLINDER SOLENOID  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly C --  REPLACE HYDRAULIC BRAKE BOOSTER ASSEMBLY  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Hydraulic Brake Booster/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. dtcs.description: BRAKE CONTROL / DYNAMIC CONTROL SYSTEMS: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM: C1401, C1271, C1272, C1402: Front Speed Sensor RH Malfunction

Front Speed Sensor LH Malfunction The speed sensor detects the wheel speed and sends the appropriate signals to the skid control ECU. These signals are used for brake control. The speed sensor rotors have rows of alternating N and S magnetic poles and their magnetic fields change when the rotors turn. Each speed sensor detects that magnetic change and sends a pulse signal to the skid control ECU. HINT * DTCs C1401 and C1271 are for the front speed sensor RH. * DTCs C1402 and C1272 are for the front speed sensor LH. NOTICE: Check the speed sensor signal after cleaning or replacement See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT SPEED SENSOR INSTALLATION (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Check the speed sensor installation. OK: There is no clearance between the sensor and the front steering knuckle. The installation bolt is tightened properly. Torque 8.5 N*m (87 kgf*cm, 75 in.*lbf) Text in Illustration NG --  INSTALL FRONT SPEED SENSOR CORRECTLY OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FRONT SPEED SENSOR TIP (a) Remove the front speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal. (b) Check the speed sensor tip. OK: No scratches, oil, or foreign matter on the sensor tip. NOTICE: If no damage to the speed sensor tip is found during this inspection, do not replace the speed sensor. NG --  CLEAN OR REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT FRONT SPEED SENSOR ROTOR (a) Remove the front axle with ABS rotor bearing assembly (for 2WD:    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal, for 4WD:    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal). (b) Check the speed sensor rotor. OK: No scratches, oil, or foreign matter on the rotors. HINT The front speed sensor rotor is incorporated into the front axle with ABS rotor bearing assembly. If the front speed sensor rotor needs to be replaced, replace it together with the front axle with ABS rotor bearing assembly. NG --  CLEAN OR REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR ROTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FRONT SPEED SENSOR) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Start the engine. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Data List. ABS/VSC/TRAC (f) Check that there is no difference between the speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream and the speed value displayed on the speedometer when driving the vehicle. HINT Factors that affect the indicated vehicle speed include tire size, tire inflation and tire wear. The speed indicated on the speedometer has an allowable margin of error. This can be tested using a speedometer tester (calibrated chassis dynamometer). For details about testing and the margin of error, refer to the reference chart    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Meter / Gauge System/Operation Check. OK: The speed value output from the speed sensor displayed on the Techstream is the same as the actual vehicle speed measured using a speedometer tester (calibrated chassis dynamometer). NG --  REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

REPLACE FRONT SPEED SENSOR (a) Replace the front speed sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Wheel Speed Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Speed Sensor/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result B --  REPLACE FRONT AXLE WITH ABS ROTOR BEARING ASSEMBLY  See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly C --  REPLACE FRONT AXLE WITH ABS ROTOR BEARING ASSEMBLY  See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Wheel Hub/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly A --  END

dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Zero Point Malfunction The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for approximately 10 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. If the front wheel alignment or steering wheel position is adjusted without disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal, or if the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point is not acquired after the adjustments have been completed, the skid control ECU detects the difference between the previous zero point and newly acquired zero point and stores this DTC to indicate poor adjustment. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning is canceled by turning the ignition switch off. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION OF YAW RATE AND ACCELERATION SENSOR (a) Perform zero point calibration of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT * When the stored zero point of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor is cleared, steering angle sensor zero point is also cleared. * If the zero point and output value of the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the output value of the speed sensors are not normal, steering angle sensor zero point cannot be acquired normally even if the vehicle is driven straight ahead at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for at least 10 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION OK --  END

dtcs.description: Acceleration Sensor Power Supply Voltage Malfunction The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle's condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are stored. These DTCs are also stored when calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: * When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. dtcs.description: Acceleration Sensor Internal Circuit

Yaw Rate Sensor Internal Circuit The skid control ECU receives signals from the yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system. The yaw rate and acceleration sensor has a built-in acceleration sensor and detects the vehicle condition using 2 circuits (GL1, GL2). If there is trouble in the bus lines between the yaw rate and acceleration sensor and the CAN communication system, DTCs U0123 (malfunction in CAN communication with the yaw rate and acceleration sensor) and U0124 (malfunction in CAN communication with the acceleration sensor) are stored. These DTCs are also stored when calibration has not been completed. NOTICE: When replacing the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

Open in Stop Light Switch Circuit The master cylinder solenoid (skid control ECU) inputs stop light switch signals and the brake operation condition. The skid control ECU has an open detection circuit, which outputs this DTC when detecting an open circuit in the stop light input line or the ground line of the stop light circuit with the stop light switch off (brake pedal not depressed). NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Power Source Voltage Malfunction Steering angle sensor signals are sent to the skid control ECU via the CAN communication system. When there is a malfunction in the CAN communication system, it is detected by the steering angle sensor zero point malfunction diagnostic function. NOTICE: * Do not remove the steering angle sensor from the spiral cable. * When replacing the spiral cable, confirm that the replacement part is of the correct specification. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. HINT * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be stored even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and acceleration sensor are output together with DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and acceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Steering Angle Sensor Initialization Incomplete

Vehicle Driven with Steering Angle Sensor not Initialized The skid control ECU acquires steering angle sensor zero point every time the ignition switch is turned to ON and the vehicle is driven at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for approximately 10 seconds. The ECU also stores the previous zero point. The steering angle sensor zero point malfunction warning is canceled by turning the ignition switch off. NOTICE: * Before proceeding with the inspection, explain to the customer that the output DTC is related to temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops when the battery is removed, and confirm that this condition occurred. * When replacing the master cylinder solenoid, perform calibration See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT * When DTC U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 is output together with DTC C1439 and/or C1445, inspect and repair the trouble areas indicated by DTC U0073, U0123, U0124 and/or U0126 first See: U Code Charts/U0073/Vehicle Stability Control System. * When the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor has trouble, DTCs for the steering angle sensor may be stored even when the steering angle sensor is normal. When DTCs for the speed sensor or yaw rate and acceleration sensor are output together with other DTCs for the steering angle sensor, inspect and repair the speed sensor and yaw rate and acceleration sensor first, and then inspect and repair the steering angle sensor. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON again and check that no CAN communication system DTC is output. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 40 km/h (25 mph) and turn the steering wheel to the right and left, and then check that no speed sensor and yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT * If there is a malfunction in the speed sensor or the yaw rate and acceleration sensor, an abnormal value may be output although the steering angle sensor is normal. * If the speed sensor and the yaw rate and acceleration sensor DTCs are output simultaneously, repair the sensors and inspect the steering angle sensor. * If DTC C1439 and/or C1445 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting C --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System D --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM STEERING ANGLE SENSOR ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Drive the vehicle straight ahead at 40 km/h (25 mph) or more for at least 10 seconds. (b) Check that the centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set while driving straight ahead. HINT If the front wheel alignment and steering position are adjusted as a result of an abnormal centered position of the steering wheel, acquire the yaw rate and acceleration sensor zero point again after the adjustments are completed. OK: The centered position of the steering wheel is correctly set. NG --  ADJUST FRONT WHEEL ALIGNMENT OR STEERING POSITION  See: Maintenance/Alignment/Service and Repair/Front Wheel Alignment OK -- Continue to next step.

RECONFIRM DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Start the engine. (d) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 40 km/h (25 mph) or more and turn the steering wheel to the right and left. (e) Check if the same DTC is output    See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If DTC C1439 and/or C1445 is not output again, the DTC may have been stored due to a temporary loss of the steering angle zero calibration, such as when the power voltage drops. B --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Vehicle Stability Control System A --  END

dtcs.description: 11-C2114/14: Transmitter ID1 Operation Stop

14 - Transmitter ID4 Operation Stop The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressures of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM FORCED TRANSMISSION OF TRANSMITTER ID OF ALL WHEELS (a) Set the tire pressure to the specified value    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take approximately 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. (g) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure of each tire by at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (1) Check that tire pressure data for each tire displayed on the Techstream screen has changed. OK: Tire pressure data for each tire displayed on the Techstream changes to the value of the tire pressure. NOTICE: * It takes approximately 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then rapidly release the tire pressure and recheck it. (2) After confirming that all of the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream screen has changed, set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified values. HINT If the tire pressure data displayed on the Techstream has not changed after rechecking it, inspect for other problems (malfunction in transmission or reception function)    See: C2121. NG --  INSPECT FOR OTHER PROBLEM (MALFUNCTION IN TRANSMISSION OR RECEPTION FUNCTION)  See: C2121 OK --  END

dtcs.description: 21-C2124/24, C2181/81-C2184/84: No Signal from Transmitter ID1 in Main Mode

84 - Transmitter ID4 not Received (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the tire pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured tire pressure values with the tire pressure threshold. When the measured tire pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

OK: Facilities or devices that use similar radio frequencies are not located in or around the vehicle. HINT Radio frequencies may be interrupted due to the surroundings or devices installed by the user. NG --  CHECK IF ANY DEVICE IS INSTALLED BY USER OK -- Continue to next step.

(f) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (g) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take approximately 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. (h) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure of each tire by at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (i) Check the Data List. (j) After confirming that the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for one tire (ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. Identify the transmitter that corresponds to the DTC. dtcs.description: 26 - Transmitter ID not Received in Main Mode After all IDs are registered, DTC C2126/26 is stored in the tire pressure warning ECU and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then comes on. When the tire pressure warning ECU successfully receives information from all the transmitters whose IDs are stored in the ECU, DTC C2126/26 is cleared and the tire pressure warning light goes off. HINT If the IDs stored in the tire pressure warning ECU differ from the transmitter IDs, DTC C2126/26 is stored. Check that the IDs in the ECU are the same as the transmitter IDs. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 41-C2144/44: Transmitter ID1 Error

44 - Transmitter ID4 Error The tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are installed in the tire and wheel assemblies measure the air pressure of the tires. The measured values are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. The ECU compares the measured air pressure values with the air pressure threshold. When the measured air pressure value is less than this threshold, the NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (g) Check the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (h) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure of each tire by at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. If "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data displayed on the Techstream (ID1 to ID4) does not change, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter corresponding to the unchanged "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data is the cause of the output DTC. HINT * Identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter by repeatedly decreasing the tire pressure for each tire. * Record which "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) corresponds to which tire. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take approximately 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. (i) Check the Data List. Result NOTICE: * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (j) When an "ID Inflation Pressure" data value (one of ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (k) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. (l) Set the tire pressures of all tires to the specified values    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valve and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 65-C2168/68: Abnormal Temperature Inside ID1 Tire

68 - Abnormal Temperature Inside ID4 Tire Each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter measures the internal temperature of its tire as well as the tire pressure and transmits the information to the tire pressure warning ECU along with the transmitter ID. If the measured temperature is out of the specified range, the tire pressure warning ECU recognizes it as a malfunction and stores DTCs, and the tire pressure warning light blinks for 1 minute and then remains on. HINT It is necessary to perform the procedure to identify the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter that is malfunctioning because it cannot be identified by the output DTC. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TIRES (a) Check that the tires are not flat and have no indication of air pressure drop. OK: Tires are normal. HINT If a tire is damaged, the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter may also have been damaged at the same time. NG --  REPLACE TIRE AND TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER OK -- Continue to next step.

IDENTIFY TRANSMITTER CORRESPONDING TO DTC (a) Set the tire pressure of all tires to the specified value    See: Maintenance/Wheels and Tires/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (e) Turn the Techstream on. (f) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (g) Check the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data for each wheel using the Techstream. (h) Rapidly reduce the tire pressure of each tire by at least 40 kPa (0.4 kgf/cm2, 5.8 psi) within 30 seconds. (i) Check the Data List. Tire Pressure Monitor HINT *: It may take approximately 2 or 3 minutes until the values are displayed. If the values are not displayed after a few minutes, perform according to the inspection procedure for DTCs C2121/21 to C2124/24    See: C2121. Result NOTICE: * It takes approximately 2 or 3 minutes to display the updated tire pressure data. * When "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data has not changed, reset the tire pressure to the appropriate specified value and rotate the tire 90 to 270°. Then forcibly transmit the transmitter ID and recheck. * Record the transmitter ID and position of transmitters that are normal. (j) When an "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data value (ID1 to ID4) has changed, repeat this procedure for each tire. (k) When all of the "ID Tire Inflation Pressure" data (ID1 to ID4) has changed, identify the malfunctioning tire pressure warning valve and transmitter using the recorded ID numbers and output DTC. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING VALVE AND TRANSMITTER (a) Replace the identified tire pressure warning valve and transmitter with a new one    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. HINT * Before installing a new tire pressure warning valve and transmitter, read and write down its transmitter ID. * The IDs for the tire pressure warning valves and transmitters that are not replaced should be checked using the Techstream and recorded. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION OF TRANSMITTER ID (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 71 - Transmitter ID not Registered The ID of each tire pressure warning valve and transmitter is registered to the tire pressure warning ECU. When the ECU detects that a transmitter ID code is not registered in the ECU, this DTC is stored. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and note them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CONFIRM REGISTRATION CONDITION (REGISTERED ID CODES) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Check the values by referring to the table below. Tire Pressure Monitor Result B --  CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST) A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM REGISTRATION (TRANSMITTER ID) (a) Register the transmitter IDs for all the wheels    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CONFIRM TIRE INFLATION PRESSURE (DATA LIST)   See: C2121 B --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END

dtcs.description: 73 - Vehicle Speed or Engine Speed Signal Malfunction The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter and an engine speed signal from the ECM. The tire pressure NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM SIGNAL CHECK (VEHICLE SPEED SIGNAL AND ENGINE SPEED SIGNAL) (a) Enter signal check mode in the Test Mode Procedure    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Test Mode Procedure. (b) Drive the vehicle at 20 km/h (12 mph) or more for 10 seconds to clear DTCs C2191/91 and C2194/94. (c) Check the test mode DTCs. Result HINT DTCs C2181/81 to C2184/84 (Transmitter ID not Received) are output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  GO TO DTC C2191/91  See: C2191 C --  GO TO DTC C2194/94  See: C2194 A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: 76 - Receiver Error The signals are transmitted to the tire pressure warning antenna and receiver on the body as radio waves and then sent to the tire pressure warning ECU. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - RECEIVER) (a) Disconnect the F7 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the V3 receiver connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: 91 - Vehicle Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode and cleared when a vehicle speed signal of 20 km/h (12 mph) is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is stored only in test mode. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (VEHICLE SPEED) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: Vehicle speed indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. NG --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (SPD SIGNAL) OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (SPD SIGNAL) (a) Disconnect the F7 ECU connector. (b) Jack up the vehicle. (c) Move the shift the lever to N. (d) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (e) Check the waveform of the ECU connector using an oscilloscope while turning any of the wheels slowly. OK: Tool Setting HINT The wavelength becomes shorter as the wheels are rotated faster. Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ECU - COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the F7 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the F14 meter connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  GO TO METER/GAUGE SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Meter / Gauge System/How To Proceed With

dtcs.description: 94 - Engine Speed Signal Error (Test Mode DTC) The tire pressure warning ECU receives an engine speed signal from the ECM. This DTC is stored upon entering test mode and cleared when an engine speed signal of 1000 rpm is detected for 3 seconds or more. This DTC is stored only in test mode. NOTICE: * When replacing the tire pressure warning ECU, read the transmitter IDs stored in the old ECU using the Techstream and write them down before removal. * It is necessary to perform registration of the transmitter IDs into the tire pressure warning ECU after the ECU and/or the tire pressure warning valve and transmitter has been replaced See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ENGINE SPEED) (a) Turn the ignition switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Turn the Techstream on. (e) Enter the following menus: Chassis / Tire Pressure Monitor / Data List. (f) Check that the values indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. Tire Pressure Monitor OK: Engine speed indicated on the Techstream and on the combination meter are the same. NG --  INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (TACH SIGNAL) OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

INSPECT TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (TACH SIGNAL) (a) Disconnect the F7 ECU connector. (b) Start the engine. (c) Check the waveform of the ECU connector using an oscilloscope while idling the engine. OK: Tool Setting HINT The wavelength becomes shorter as the engine speed increases. Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU - ECM) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal

(c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.
